summary.business_type: B2B SaaS / developer tool (AI agent infrastructure for SaaS products)
top_findings: No structured data (JSON-LD) anywhere on either domain
Six+ distinct pages (/, /pricing, /demo, /contact, /auth/sign-in, /auth/sign-up) share the exact same <title> and meta description
No sitemap.xml on betteragent.dev or docs.betteragent.dev (both 404)
robots.txt blocks GPTBot, ClaudeBot, Google-Extended, Applebot-Extended, CCBot, Bytespider, meta-externalagent
/demo is fully client-rendered with no SSR fallback -- 9 words of server text, no <h1>, duplicate meta
quick_wins: Write unique titles/descriptions per page (~10 min/page, highest leverage available)
Add app/sitemap.ts and reference it in robots.txt
Add Open Graph + Twitter Card tags with one branded image
Add canonical tags via Next.js metadata API (alternates.canonical)
Add Organization + SoftwareApplication JSON-LD to the homepage using existing pricing data
Fix the doubled '-- BetterAgent -- BetterAgent' title suffix on /privacy and /terms

categories.name: Technical SEO
what_works: HTTPS enforced with HSTS (max-age=63072000)
Clean, semantic URLs (/pricing, /contact, /demo, /privacy, /terms)
robots.txt explicitly allows general search crawling (Content-Signal: search=yes)
Mobile viewport meta present on every page
Pages are Next.js-prerendered and edge-cached (x-vercel-cache: HIT, cf-cache-status)
Custom 404 page returns proper 404 status code with helpful copy and links

findings.title: No sitemap.xml on either domain
findings.description: https://betteragent.dev/sitemap.xml and https://docs.betteragent.dev/sitemap.xml both resolve to the site's not-found page (soft 404, HTTP 404 confirmed). No app/sitemap.ts exists in the codebase. No Sitemap: directive in either robots.txt.
findings.recommendation: Add app/sitemap.ts exporting a MetadataRoute.Sitemap with all public routes (/, /pricing, /demo, /contact, /privacy, /terms); add an equivalent for the Nextra docs app; reference both via Sitemap: lines in robots.txt.

findings.title: No canonical tags on any page
findings.description: None of the 8 crawled pages (/, /pricing, /demo, /contact, /privacy, /terms, /auth/sign-in, /auth/sign-up) emit a <link rel="canonical">. This matters more here because the apex domain redirects to www.betteragent.dev, and metadataBase in app/layout.tsx is set to the apex (https://betteragent.dev) -- a mismatch with the domain actually serving content.
findings.recommendation: Set alternates.canonical per page via the Next.js Metadata API, and align metadataBase with whichever host (apex or www) is the canonical one.

findings.title: robots.txt blocks every major AI crawler
findings.description: Cloudflare's managed robots.txt content disallows GPTBot, ClaudeBot, Google-Extended, Applebot-Extended, CCBot, Bytespider, meta-externalagent, Amazonbot, and CloudflareBrowserRenderingCrawler, with Content-Signal: ai-train=no set globally. This looks like Cloudflare's default AI-bot ruleset rather than a deliberate product decision.
findings.recommendation: Confirm with the team whether this is intentional. If the goal is just to prevent training on the content, ai-train=no can stay, but the explicit Disallow: / rules for ClaudeBot/GPTBot/Google-Extended also block ai-input (RAG/grounding) -- meaning the product can never surface when someone asks an AI assistant 'what should I use to add an agent to my SaaS.' Loosen to allow read/input access while keeping training disabled.

findings.title: No baseline security headers
findings.description: The homepage response has no Content-Security-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, or Permissions-Policy headers.
findings.recommendation: Add these via next.config headers() or a middleware -- low effort, and increasingly factored into some search trust/quality signals.

findings.title: Apex-to-www redirect uses a temporary (307) status
findings.description: https://betteragent.dev/ responds with HTTP 307 to https://www.betteragent.dev/ instead of a permanent redirect.
findings.recommendation: Switch to a 301/308 permanent redirect so link equity consolidates onto the canonical host and crawlers don't keep re-checking the apex.

categories.name: Content Quality
what_works: Homepage copy is specific and avoids generic SaaS filler ('it reads your routes and server actions, generates the schemas...')
Pricing page clearly explains the credit-based billing model with a full plan comparison
Docs subdomain (Nextra) has clean, purpose-built MDX content: quickstart, AI setup, CLI reference, tool files, components

findings.title: /demo renders almost no content server-side
findings.description: /demo's server-rendered HTML contains only ~9 words of text (the page title, duplicated from the homepage) versus ~1,000 words on the homepage. The page is a bare <Suspense><VariantSwitcher /></Suspense> client component with no metadata export, no <h1>, and no static fallback content.
findings.recommendation: Add a server-rendered heading, short description, and static preview/screenshot as a Suspense fallback so the page has indexable content even before the client-side variant switcher hydrates.

findings.title: No authority/E-E-A-T signal anywhere in the site
findings.description: No About, team, founder, case-study, or testimonial page exists in the link graph. There's no human-authorship or credibility signal beyond the product copy itself.
findings.recommendation: Add a short About/team page, and surface at least one customer quote or usage stat once available.

findings.title: No blog or long-tail educational content
findings.description: /blog 404s; there is no content marketing surface beyond the docs.
findings.recommendation: Not urgent pre-PMF, but flag as a growth lever -- the docs subdomain is already structured well enough to extend with guides.

categories.name: On-Page SEO
what_works: Single <h1> present on most pages (/, /pricing, /contact)
lang="en" correctly set on <html>
Heading hierarchy is mostly clean on the homepage (one H1, H2 sections, H3 subsections)
Internal navigation links to all key public routes (pricing, demo, contact, privacy, terms, sign-in, sign-up) plus the docs subdomain

findings.title: Six pages share an identical title and meta description
findings.description: /, /pricing, /demo, /contact, /auth/sign-in, and /auth/sign-up all serve the exact same <title>BetterAgent -- the agent layer your SaaS is missing</title> and identical meta description, because only app/layout.tsx defines metadata and none of these page.tsx files override it.
findings.recommendation: Add a page-level `export const metadata` (or generateMetadata) to each of pricing/page.tsx, demo/page.tsx, contact/page.tsx, and the two auth pages with a unique, page-specific title and description.

findings.title: Doubled brand suffix on /privacy and /terms titles
findings.description: Both pages set their own title to '...-- BetterAgent' while the root layout's title.template ('%s -- BetterAgent') also appends the suffix, producing 'Privacy Policy -- BetterAgent -- BetterAgent' and 'Terms of Service -- BetterAgent -- BetterAgent' in the rendered <title>.
findings.recommendation: Drop the manual '-- BetterAgent' suffix from these two pages' title strings and let the layout template add it once.

findings.title: Zero Open Graph or Twitter Card tags
findings.description: No og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url, twitter:card, or related tags exist on any crawled page. Links shared in Slack, X, or LinkedIn render with no title, description, or image.
findings.recommendation: Add openGraph and twitter fields to the Metadata API (can be set once at the root and overridden per page) plus a branded 1200x630 opengraph-image asset.

findings.title: /demo has no <h1>
findings.description: Same root cause as the content-quality finding -- the page has no server-rendered heading at all.
findings.recommendation: Covered by the /demo SSR fix above.

findings.title: No JSON-LD anywhere on either domain
findings.description: No application/ld+json block was found on any of the 8 crawled betteragent.dev pages or on docs.betteragent.dev. No Organization, SoftwareApplication, Product/Offer, or BreadcrumbList schema exists.
findings.recommendation: Add Organization schema sitewide (name, logo, url, sameAs) and SoftwareApplication or Product+Offer schema on /pricing, mapped directly from the existing plan comparison table -- this is a near-zero-content-change, high-leverage fix.

categories.name: Performance (CWV)
what_works: Prerendered Next.js output served via Vercel with edge caching (x-vercel-cache: HIT, x-nextjs-prerender: 1)
Cloudflare CDN in front with HTTP/3 (alt-svc h3) and gzip compression
Homepage TTFB ~260ms and full response in ~270ms from a cold curl, consistent with edge-cached delivery
Reasonable HTML payload (~108KB uncompressed) for a content-rich landing page

findings.title: No lab-measured Core Web Vitals available
findings.description: PageSpeed Insights/CrUX API access is not configured in this environment, so LCP/INP/CLS are not directly measured here. The score above is a heuristic based on response headers, caching behavior, and architecture, not a Lighthouse run.
findings.recommendation: Run PageSpeed Insights manually (or configure a GOOGLE_API_KEY for python scripts/google_report.py) to get real LCP/INP/CLS numbers, especially given the homepage loads 16+ separate JS chunks.

categories.name: AI Search Readiness
what_works: Docs subdomain content (quickstart, AI setup, CLI reference, tool files, components) is clean, well-segmented MDX -- good raw material for citation once accessible
Page copy itself is concrete and specific rather than vague marketing language, which tends to extract well into AI answers when crawlable

findings.title: Every major AI assistant/answer-engine crawler is blocked
findings.description: GPTBot, ClaudeBot, Google-Extended, Applebot-Extended, CCBot, Bytespider, and meta-externalagent are all disallowed in robots.txt. For a product whose entire pitch is 'the agent layer your SaaS is missing,' this means it cannot surface when a developer asks ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini what to use to add an agent to their product.
findings.recommendation: Allow ai-input access for these crawlers (RAG/grounding at query time) even if ai-train=no is kept for IP reasons -- the two are independently controllable via Content-Signal.

findings.title: No llms.txt on either domain
findings.description: Neither betteragent.dev nor docs.betteragent.dev serves an llms.txt file (both 404).
findings.recommendation: Add an llms.txt at the docs subdomain root pointing at the existing quickstart/ai-setup/cli/tools/components pages -- low effort given the content already exists.

categories.name: Images
what_works: No broken or oversized raster images -- the marketing pages are effectively zero- (icon/SVG-driven design)
A 512x512 icon.png is correctly referenced via <link rel="icon">

findings.title: No og:image / social preview image
findings.description: No opengraph-image route or static asset exists -- confirmed via /opengraph-image returning 404. Shared links have no visual preview.
findings.recommendation: Add app/opengraph-image.tsx (or a static 1200x630 PNG) using Next.js's built-in OG image generation.

findings.title: No favicon.ico, apple-touch-icon, or manifest.json
findings.description: /favicon.ico, /apple-touch-icon.png, and /manifest.json all 404 (after following the apex->www redirect). Only the 512x512 icon.png is served.
findings.recommendation: Add a favicon.ico fallback for older clients, an apple-touch-icon for iOS home-screen bookmarking, and a basic manifest.json if any PWA-like behavior is desired.

phases.name: Phase 1: Critical Fixes
items: Write unique <title> + meta description for /pricing, /demo, /contact, /auth/sign-in, /auth/sign-up (currently all identical to the homepage)
Add app/sitemap.ts for betteragent.dev and an equivalent for docs.betteragent.dev; add Sitemap: lines to both robots.txt files
Server-render real content (heading, description, static preview) on /demo -- currently ~9 words of text, fully client-only
Decide and fix the AI-crawler block in robots.txt -- allow ai-input for ClaudeBot/GPTBot/Google-Extended at minimum, even if ai-train=no is kept
Fix the doubled '-- BetterAgent -- BetterAgent' title suffix on /privacy and /terms

phases.name: Phase 2: High-Impact Improvements
items: Add canonical tags sitewide via Next.js alternates.canonical, and align metadataBase with the actual canonical host
Add Open Graph + Twitter Card tags with one branded 1200x630 image (none exist today)
Add Organization + SoftwareApplication/Product JSON-LD -- map directly from the existing /pricing comparison table
Add llms.txt pointing into docs.betteragent.dev (quickstart, ai-setup, cli, tools, components)

phases.name: Phase 3: Content & Authority
items: Add an About/company page (founder/team info -- zero authority signal exists today)
Add at least one customer testimonial or usage stat to the homepage
Add baseline security headers (CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy)

phases.name: Phase 4: Monitoring & Iteration
items: Run PageSpeed Insights/CrUX with a Google API key for real Core Web Vitals (current Performance score is a heuristic, not measured)
Consider extending docs with guides/blog content for long-tail and AI-citation surface area once core priorities allow
